text.title: Embedding Caching Core Rules
text.summary: 4 testable rules + 2 terminal conclusions every embedding-cache implementation must enforce.

rule.statement: Cache key uses SHA-256 of `model_name + ":" + model_version + ":" + text`. MD5 rejected.
rule.rationale: MD5 collisions exist in practice; at billion-doc scale a collision returns the wrong embedding silently.

rule.statement: `model_version` is part of the key. Provider model bumps invalidate old cache entries automatically.
rule.rationale: Providers silently update weights; un-versioned keys serve stale vectors.

rule.statement: TTL ≤ days-to-model-deprecation (or 365 days, whichever is shorter). On model deprecation event, full cache flush for that model.
rule.rationale: Cache outliving the model serves vectors against a deleted decoder; correctness bug.

rule.statement: Per-call cache_hit metric emitted. Cache_hit_rate < 5% over 30 days triggers re-evaluation of caching.
rule.rationale: Low hit rate = cache overhead exceeds savings.

rule.statement: Repeated-text rate >5% AND backend available → wire cache.
One-shot embed OR <5% repeat rate OR no backend → skip; direct embed.
